urn:contentItem:6JK4-WBY3-S0GM-T14S-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ PILLSBURY CREATES BOSTON HUB TO TAP INNOVATION CORRIDOR

On 12th May 2026, Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman announced it would open an office in Boston, expanding its footprint in a leading market for law and business. The launch will begin with a team of five senior lawyers, including a litigator recruited from Latham & Watkins.Leading the effort is Robert Gilbert, who joined Pillsbury after seven years at Latham. He specializes in representing policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters and has advised Boston-area clients such as Harvard University, UniFirst Corporation, and Northland Investment Corporation. Gilbert will help establish the Boston office with Pillsbury leaders Michael Zeliger, Andrew Troop, Don Lonczak, and David Simons.David Dekker, chair and CEO, described the move as a strategic step for the firm in a city known for its startup culture and world-class research institutions. He said, "Pillsbury has long represented clients headquartered in the Boston area and those connected to the Boston-Cambridge innovation ecosystem. Opening a Boston office is a natural next step given our capabilities and relationships in the tech, life sciences, healthcare, and financial industries in Boston and around the world." With this expansion, Pillsbury reaches 17 locations in the United States and 25 worldwide. The Boston arrival aligns with a broader trend of major law firms staking a presence in the city to capitalize on Boston's thriving technology, life sciences, and financial services sectors. Recent entrants include Reed Smith in March, along with rivals such as Freshfields and Simpson Thacher & Bartlett.Looking ahead, the firm aims to deepen its engagement with local clients and industries, strengthening its Boston footprint and its global platform in technology, life sciences, healthcare, and finance.

+ MORGAN LEWIS EXPANDS BENEFITS TEAM WITH PILLSBURY HIRE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has announced the addition of a prominent attorney from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP to its Employee Benefits team, a strategic step to address the growing complexities in benefits law and the demand for expert guidance.The attorney's multi-faceted background covers compliance and regulatory issues within employee benefits, and is expected to broaden the firm's capabilities in advising clients on benefit plans and pension laws.Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire aligns with Morgan Lewis' broader push to strengthen its offerings in employee benefits amid regulatory shifts and an increasingly competitive legal market.Pillsbury has seen several of its attorneys move to other firms in recent years, and Morgan Lewis notes that the new hire will bring important regulatory insight to navigate evolving requirements.The firm has a longstanding reputation for depth in its practice areas, and leaders say the addition will help deliver more specialized services tailored to client needs.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST-PERKINS COIE MERGER CLEARED BY PARTNERS, CLOSE TARGET SET FOR Q3

Partner votes have backed the Ashurst and Perkins Coie merger, with the approval announced on 13th April 2026 and a closing target in the third quarter, subject to customary conditions.The move underscores a shared commitment to building a forward looking global firm and confidence in growth and in delivering premier legal services in a fast changing market.Paul Jenkins, CEO of Ashurst, said the two firms are well aligned and their combination will broaden client offerings and scale cross border capability.The combined group will number about 3,000 lawyers across 52 offices in 32 countries, with major hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York, and a focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services.The practice areas, together with a revenue base of around USD 2.8 billion, bring extensive international experience spanning Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. Leadership will be shared by Bill Malley and Paul Jenkins as global co-CEOs, with Ashurst global chair Karen Davies and Perkins Coie corporate practice chair Brian Eiting serving as co-chairs.The combination is expected to sharpen the firms' competitive edge and position the new entity for sustained growth in the evolving global legal market.

+ ASHURST AND PERKINS COIE AGREE TO STRATEGIC COMBINATION, LAUNCH ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, signaling broad support for a deal that pairs complementary geographic reach, sector depth, and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will be a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion and around 3,000 lawyers spread across more than 50 offices worldwide. The firm will run flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York, underscoring its expansive footprint across key markets. It will be well positioned to serve clients in technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, described the vote as recognition that the merger will create a differentiated platform with scale, sector depth, and technology leadership to meet cross-border client needs. 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will yield a differentiated global platform.'Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, stressed the alignment and ambition behind the move, noting that the combination will unlock complementary strengths across sectors and practice areas that will benefit clients. 'This vote confirms our joint ambition and the compelling platform it creates for top legal talent,' he said.The merger remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026, with the new firm aiming to navigate the evolving global market and deliver enhanced services to clients.

+ PERKINS COIE RE-ESTABLISHES MAINLAND CHINA PRESENCE WITH SHENZHEN HUB

Perkins Coie is re-entering China with a Shenzhen representative office, inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026, marking a renewed foothold in the market. The move follows the 2024 closure of its Beijing and Shanghai offices and signals a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to tap the city's rapid growth as a tech and manufacturing hub. The office will be led by Bing Ai, chief representative and managing partner, and Wei Yuan, representative and litigation partner; both are US-trained and bring deep experience serving technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management in sectors such as semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, says the Shenzhen move underscores the firm's commitment to cross-border US legal expertise and helping clients navigate global markets amid rising cross-border challenges. The development comes as several international firms push deeper into Shenzhen's Greater Bay Area, and Perkins Coie weighs a potential merger with UK-based Ashurst that could create a top-20 global firm with projected annual revenues around $2.7 billion.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY RAISES $60 MILLION IN SERIES B LED BY LUX CAPITAL AND INDEX VENTURES, WITH COOLEY AMONG BACKERS

Crosby, the contract review start-up, announced it has closed a $60 million Series B on April 3, 2026, its third capital raise in under a year, with Cooley LLP among the backers.The round was co-led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors, Bain Capital Ventures and angel investor Elad Gil.The fresh capital will fund product development and help Crosby scale to serve a growing roster of clients, including Clay, Rogo and Ramp.Crosby uses artificial intelligence paired with legal expertise to accelerate contract reviews, and the company says the value of negotiated contracts now tops $1 billion, up from roughly $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year.In a blog post, Crosby stated, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The company has rolled out simulations of counterparty responses to contract redlines, voice agents that negotiate on clients’ behalf, and a client-facing platform to oversee Crosby’s work.Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan. Daniels, a former Cooley associate, and Sarihan, a former Google software engineer and Ramp tech lead, have steered Crosby toward a future of faster contract negotiation with the support of investors including Cooley and other leading firms.

+ COOLEY TAPS FRESHFIELDS BRUCKHAUS DERINGER ALUMNI TO BOOST TECH PRACTICE

Cooley LLP is expanding its technology practice by recruiting several attorneys who previously worked at Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer, signaling a strategic effort to strengthen its tech capabilities and stay competitive in a dynamic legal market.As of 23rd November 2025, the firm’s latest recruitment drive highlights ongoing work to broaden technology offerings in a rapidly evolving sector. The addition of Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is anticipated to bolster the existing practice and help attract clients seeking specialized tech law guidance.The incoming team members bring broad experience, advising notable clients on IP, data protection, and corporate tech transactions. Their know-how is expected to augment Cooley’s well-established venture capital and startup advisory practice, further positioning the firm as a leading tech law player.The hire reflects a broader market trend toward poaching top talent to meet demands of a fast-paced, tech-driven environment. With tech-related legal challenges growing more complex, specialized knowledge is increasingly vital, making these hires timely.Through this expansion, Cooley seeks to reinforce its standing as a go-to tech law firm, supporting its innovation-driven growth. As integration proceeds, stakeholders will watch how these changes affect Cooley's market position and client services in the near term.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ REED SMITH GUIDES FOUNDER THROUGH £11 MILLION SOFTWARE TAKEOVER

ReedSmith LLP played a central role in the £11 million acquisition of a software business by its founder, with completion announced on 8th of May 2026. The transaction demonstrates the firm's capability to manage complex corporate deals in the technology space.The founder's buyout is expected to bolster operations and unlock new opportunities for innovation within the sector.Reed Smith provided comprehensive legal guidance throughout, ensuring regulatory compliance and facilitating negotiations between the parties, reflecting the firm's strategy of supporting entrepreneurial ventures through mergers and acquisitions.Analysts note that such strategic acquisitions are on the rise as firms seek competitive advantage, and Reed Smith's handling of this deal positions the firm as a trusted advisor in the tech industry.The deal could reshape the landscape as the founder plans to leverage assets and integrate new technologies to expand market reach, with Reed Smith backing the initiative and a robust legal framework.The firm's successful navigation of this takeover reinforces its reputation for tailored solutions for technology clients and suggests continued investor confidence as the software company enters a new phase under the founder's leadership.

+ REED SMITH EXPANDS DC REAL ESTATE TEAM WITH STINSON FINANCE ATTORNEY

ReedSmith LLP announced the addition of a real estate finance attorney from Stinson LLP to its Washington, D.C. office, reinforcing its capabilities in a robust market. The hire brings extensive experience in real estate financing, acquisition and development, including work on complex transactions for lenders, developers and borrowers.The move complements Reed Smith's existing real estate offerings in DC and positions the firm to better serve clients navigating evolving regulatory requirements and market dynamics.The D.C. office has long been a hub for the firm's real estate practice, and the new attorney is expected to strengthen the client roster and project portfolio.As of 7th May 2026, Reed Smith notes the market demand for sophisticated real estate counsel remains high, underscoring the strategic value of the hire.

+ REED SMITH EXPANDS LA PRIVATE EQUITY TEAM WITH SENIOR PARTNER APPOINTMENT

ReedSmith LLP has expanded its private equity practice with the appointment of a new partner in its Los Angeles office, a move aimed at strengthening service to investment firms and corporate clients in mergers and acquisitions.The partner joins on 4th May 2026 and brings substantial experience in private equity transactions and related advisory services, supporting the firm's effort to navigate complex deals.The Los Angeles market continues to attract capital inflows into private equity, and the firm expects to leverage the new partner's expertise to tailor solutions for its clients.The addition reinforces Reed Smith's reputation for high-quality legal services and long-term client relationships as the private equity landscape evolves and demands more sophisticated guidance on investment structures and regulatory matters.The firm is well-positioned to capitalize on growth opportunities in private equity with this hire.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI P.C. [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOORBIT RAISES $13.2 MILLION SEED FINANCING WITH WILSON SONSINI AS COUNSEL

BioOrbit has closed a seed financing of $13.2 million (£9.8 million), with counsel from Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. The round was co-led by LocalGlobe and BREEGA, with participation from Seedcamp, Auxxo Female Catalyst Fund, Type One Ventures, 7percent Ventures and other investors.The funding was announced on 30th April 2026.BioOrbit's leadership includes Cambridge scientist Dr. Katie King and oncology researcher Dr. Leonor Teles, who are pursuing a space-based pharmaceuticals factory within the next decade. The company is developing hardware to enable large-scale crystallization of protein drugs, an essential step in biopharmaceutical manufacturing.Wilson Sonsini's team, led by Daniel Glazer and supported by Jose Campos, Rashad Mohammed and Sophia Kunthara, provided corporate and emerging-company counsel to BioOrbit during the financing, positioning the startup for its ambitious space-manufacturing plans.The investment highlights growing interest in life sciences innovations for space applications, a trend the firm says is drawing increasing attention from investors. BioOrbit also directs stakeholders to LinkedIn for updates and to UKTN for additional coverage.

+ OFF PISTE CAPITAL CLOSES FUND I WITH WILSON SONSINI COUNSEL, SIGNALS INFRASTRUCTURE-FOCUSED VC PATH

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. said it advised Off Piste Capital on the launch of its first fund, Off Piste Fund I. The new venture capital firm, focused on backing innovative founders, closed the fund on 29th April 2026 with a select group of strategic limited partners, including Edition Ventures.Off Piste Fund I aims to invest in companies building the systems and infrastructure for the next era. The fund has already deployed capital into four startups: Stendr, a Norway-based maker of AI-driven command and control tech for counter-drone operations; Objection, a UK firm building accountability infrastructure for public information; Beyond Reach Labs, a US YC-backed outfit creating deployable space structures; and Octet, a UK company developing cryptographic proofs of physical location.Legal work was led by Wilson Sonsini partners and associates including Bradley Doline, Alice Cao, and Stephanie Yasay, who guided fund formation, regulatory considerations, and closing mechanics. The cooperation highlights Wilson Sonsini's active role in supporting emerging venture activity and its growing position in the technology and infrastructure investment ecosystem.With Fund I now active Off Piste plans follow-on investments across sectors where next-generation systems and infrastructure enable growth. The partnership with Wilson Sonsini and the presence of Edition Ventures as a backer underscore a broader industry interest in technology-enabled infrastructure.Interested observers can view Off Piste's founder's LinkedIn post or EU-Startups coverage for more context about the launch and strategy.

+ KEN SANOCKI JOINS WILSON SONSINI AS ECS PARTNER, BOOSTING ENERGY-TRANSITION FINANCING

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati named Ken Sanocki a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions practice, expanding the firm's capacity to guide clients through complex energy-infrastructure financing during the energy transition.Sanocki previously served as vice president of legal at Sunrun, the largest residential solar and storage provider in the United States, where he oversaw all project-finance matters, including tax equity financings and debt structures for large-scale distributed-energy projects. Since 5th May 2026, he has led more than 50 tax equity financings totaling over $10 billion across diverse distributed-energy portfolios.Doug Clark, the firm's managing partner, commented, "Ken has operated at the center of the energy transition, helping shape the financing structures that have enabled distributed energy to scale into mainstream infrastructure." His expertise in tax equity and structured financings, paired with commercial instincts, will help clients navigate energy-financing complexities.At Wilson Sonsini, Sanocki will advise companies, sponsors, and investors on structured and project finance across renewable energy, storage, and climate infrastructure, and support capital formation and strategic transactions, including asset-portfolio development and preparation for IPOs or M&A. The firm has been expanding its clean-energy advisory capabilities through partner additions who specialize in structured finance and regulatory matters.Sanocki's background also includes serving as senior counsel for global structured and project finance at SunEdison and beginning his career as a project-finance associate at Milbank. He earned a J.D. from NYU School of Law and a B.A. from Boston College. His arrival, alongside other recent partner hires, signals Wilson Sonsini's strengthened position in sophisticated energy-transition finance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN GUIDES $1.3 BILLION CLOSURE FOR S3 MULTIFAMILY FUND

Sidley Austin has advised on the closure of a $1.3 billion multifamily-focused S3 fund, marking a notable milestone for the real estate investment sector. The announcement, dated 7th May 2026, underscores growing demand for multifamily housing driven by demographic shifts and urbanization. The S3 vehicle is designed to target multifamily properties and is expected to attract robust investor interest as housing needs evolve. With urban areas seeing higher population density, multifamily assets are viewed as stable and lucrative, and Sidley's work on its formation and compliance highlights the firm's strengths in real estate financing. Investors are increasingly drawn to multifamily investments because of their relative resilience in downturns. The S3 fund aims to target properties in high-demand markets with solid rental fundamentals, leveraging urban growth trends to attract capital. Sidley Austin's advisory role helps ensure that the fund aligns with investor expectations and regulatory standards, thereby enhancing its appeal to potential capital partners. The close demonstrates Sidley Austin's ability to facilitate significant real estate investments, reinforcing the firm's standing to continue its advisory leadership in the multifamily segment. This milestone reinforces Sidley Austin's position as a leading advisor to real estate financiers and developers.

+ TELIX-REGENERON RADIOPHARMACEUTICAL PACT VALUED UP TO USD 2.1 BILLION; SIDLEY AUSTIN ADVISES TELIX

Sidley Austin is advising Telix Pharmaceuticals on its strategic collaboration with Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, a partnership valued at up to USD 2.1 billion. The arrangement will see Telix and Regeneron co-developing and co-commercialising next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies under a 50:50 cost-and-profit-sharing model, with Telix receiving USD 40 million upfront for four initial programmes. Disclosed on 21st April 2026, the deal centers on joint governance and shared development and commercialization responsibilities, aiming to leverage both companies' oncology platforms. Telix is pursuing an aggressive expansion in radiopharmaceuticals, seeking to broaden its footprint in cancer care through partnerships that combine Regeneron's oncology capabilities with Telix's radiopharmaceutical pipeline. Joshua Hofheimer, a Sidley partner, commented that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The alliance is positioned to accelerate radiopharmaceutical development while balancing regulatory and commercial considerations. Industry observers note that the deal aligns with a broader wave of M&A activity in the biopharma space, and Sidley Austin continues to build its reputation as a premier adviser on cross-border collaborations in a changing regulatory environment.

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN EXPANDS CITY FOOTPRINT AS REVENUE RISES 33% ON HIRING DRIVE

Sidley Austin is reporting a 33% increase in City revenue, credited to a targeted hiring push as of 14th April 2026. The growth underscores Sidley's expanded City footprint following recruitment of senior lawyers and other specialists. Executives say the hires have diversified practice areas and improved client service, lifting billable hours and retention. Leadership intends to refine hiring practices and broaden capabilities to stay ahead in a competitive market. Industry observers see the firm's approach as a potential model for other firms navigating tighter margins and evolving client needs.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ MORRISON FOERSTER STRENGTHENS GLOBAL TECHNOLOGY TRANSACTIONS WITH PIERIDES, HOLDER, BELL

Morrison Foerster announced the addition of partners Mike Pierides, Will Holder, and Oliver Bell to its global technology transactions and technology-focused M&A platform, expanding a team that already covers outsourcing, data privacy, cybersecurity, digital transformation, and complex commercial matters. The trio brings more than 55 years of combined experience, strengthening the firm's cross-border capabilities as it grows its London presence. The firm made this announcement on the 9th of May, 2026.Pierides' practice centers on technology and commercial transactions, including outsourcing, data privacy and cybersecurity, GPU-as-a-service agreements, licensing arrangements, and technology-as-a-service mandates. He advises clients across AI, data centers, fintech, aviation, telecommunications, and financial services, reflecting Morrison Foerster's focus on AI-driven technology transactions.Holder enhances the technology and media-focused M&A practice, covering public and private M&A, private equity transactions, joint ventures, and corporate matters, with experience spanning technology, music, sports, media, fintech, and consumer sectors.Bell is set to join in the coming weeks, bringing experience in technology-led transactions, large-scale IT outsourcing agreements, and cybersecurity matters.The firm said the hires bolster cross-border technology transactions and AI-related capabilities and support its London office growth strategy. They represent the eighth, ninth, and tenth partner additions in Morrison Foerster's London office over the past year, underscoring client demand across AI, fintech, data centers, technology infrastructure, and digital transformation.Eric McCrath, Chair, stated that technology is at the heart of Morrison Foerster's work and the appointments reflect continued investment in core tech M&A and technology transactions capabilities. Andrew Boyd, London office managing partner, added that the new partners' practices align with the firm's strengths and will create valuable synergies.

+ SENATE BANS SENATORS AND STAFF FROM PREDICTION MARKETS, MORRISON & FOERSTER SAYS

In a move that could reshape financial markets, the Senate on 29th April 2026 unanimously approved a resolution barring Senators and their staff from participating in prediction markets. Morrison & Foerster characterizes the action as part of a broader push to tighten oversight of these platforms, which have drawn both enthusiasm and skepticism.Shortly after the resolution, Senators Kristen Gillibrand (D-NY) and Dave McCormick (R-PA) introduced legislation to prevent government officials from trading on prediction markets. The draft bill would establish insider trading standards, require anti-money-laundering measures, and create an independent Office of the Retail Advocate within the CFTC, signaling deeper regulatory controls over the space.Kalshi, a regulated exchange for prediction markets, announced features to curb underage access, including mandatory face ID for users and two-factor authentication for parents. Kalshi says the updates align with the legislative framework proposed by the lawmakers and could bolster consumer trust in these markets.The Senate Commerce, Science, and Transportation Committee is scheduled to hold a hearing on 20th May 2026 to address prediction markets and sports betting. Former Representative Patrick McHenry is set to testify along with industry representatives, a sign that the event could influence future policy.CFTC Chairman Michael Selig has defended the commission’s exclusive jurisdiction over prediction markets in a letter to The Wall Street Journal, describing them as innovative financial products rather than gambling sites. Morrison & Foerster notes that this defense is important for maintaining legitimacy as lawmakers scrutinize the sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ENTER CLOSES $100M SERIES B LED BY FOUNDERS FUND; GREENBERG TRAURIG GUIDES LANDMARK ROUND

SAO PAULO, May 8, 2026 — Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented Enter, a Brazil-based artificial intelligence legal technology company, in its Series B financing that exceeded $100 million and values Enter at about $1.2 billion, making it the first AI unicorn in Latin America. The round was led by Founders Fund, with notable participation from Sequoia Capital, Ribbit Capital, Kaszek, Atlantico and ONEVC. Enter, founded in 2023, has built a client roster including Airbnb, Nubank, Mercado Libre and Latam Airlines, among more than 40 others. Enter's platform uses advanced AI to automate tasks in litigation workflows, including evidence discovery, drafting responses and negotiating settlements, with all work reviewed by Enter's network of legal professionals. The Greenberg Traurig deal team was led by Matthew Squires, a shareholder in Corporate and the Latin America practice, and included Maurice Guttmann, L. Frank Cordero, Agata Jacqueline Paramesvari and Nathalya Zara. Greenberg Traurig, which has about 3,100 lawyers across 51 offices, is regularly ranked among the Am Law Global 100 and emphasizes philanthropy, culture and pro bono initiatives.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG EXPANDS RESTRUCTURING CAPABILITIES IN CHICAGO WITH GERARD C. MARTIN

Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has expanded its Chicago office by naming Gerard C. Martin as a shareholder in the Restructuring & Special Situations Practice. Martin joins from Reed Smith LLP and brings a broad background in U.S. and international financing transactions from Reed Smith. His practice covers cash-flow and asset-based loans, leveraged loans, acquisition financings, complex intercreditor arrangements, and equipment and aviation finance, including aircraft and engine financings and project finance. In restructurings, he advises secured creditors and debtors in both in-court and out-of-court transactions, including debtor-in-possession financings and distressed investing. His industry experience spans manufacturing, energy, aviation, technology, health care and financial services, reinforcing Greenberg Traurig's service offerings. Martin said, "Greenberg Traurig is an ideal fit for my practice, and the firm’s platform aligns with my work in financing and restructuring," and Shari L. Heyen and Oscar N. Pinkas said his arrival strengthens the firm's capabilities. The Chicago office, with more than 200 attorneys, is positioned to leverage his expertise as clients navigate cycles in credit.

+ GT ADVISES ON 40-MW KEIHANNA DATA CENTER SALE NEAR OSAKA TO CAPITALAND ASCENDAS REIT-LED CONSORTIUM

Greenberg Traurig, LLP advised the seller in the sale of a stabilized 40-megawatt hyperscale data center in Keihanna, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan, near Osaka. The transaction closed on 7th May 2026 and represents one of the largest asset-level sales of a fully leased hyperscale facility in the Japanese market. The data center is leased to a single US hyperscale cloud provider. The buyer is a consortium led by CapitaLand Ascendas REIT, which acquired a 49% stake, while a fund affiliated with a Japanese trading company purchased the remaining 51%. The seller comprises a joint venture between a US institutional manager’s fund and a Japanese trading company fund. This deal marks Greenberg Traurig’s second hyperscale data center sale for this client in Japan, following a prior transaction completed in November. The firm emphasizes its Digital Infrastructure Industry Group, which numbers over 110 attorneys worldwide and supports data center, digital infrastructure, and telecommunications matters.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AND GOODWIN STEER ESPERION'S $1.1B TAKE-PRIVATE IN BIOTECH SECTOR SHIFT

Esperion Therapeutics Inc. has entered a $1.1 billion take-private agreement, with Sidley Austin LLP and Goodwin Procter LLP handling the transaction's legal work. The deal, announced on 1st May 2026, is designed to reposition the lipid-management specialist away from public market volatility and toward a deeper focus on its programs.The arrangement comes amid growing investor and private equity interest in biotechnology, with private ownership seen as a way to streamline operations and accelerate development.Sidley and Goodwin are recognized for their mergers-and-acquisitions expertise and have been tasked with guiding the process through governance and regulatory considerations.Industry analysts say moving to private ownership could enable longer-term planning and increased investment in R&D, with investor attention turning to Esperion's performance after the transition.The deal also spotlights the pivotal role of major law firms in facilitating large biotech transactions, potentially signaling more private-market activity in the sector.

+ FBT GIBBONS ADDS VENTURE CAPITAL ATTORNEY FROM GOODWIN PROCTER LLP

On 21st April 2026, FBT Gibbons confirmed the recruitment of a venture capital attorney from Goodwin Procter LLP to its New Jersey office. The hire strengthens the firm's venture capital practice as the sector grows. The incoming attorney brings broad experience from Goodwin Procter LLP, known for its robust venture capital work spanning startups and established companies. FBT Gibbons says the addition will help clients raise capital and navigate complex investment environments. The move reflects a wider industry trend of firms expanding niche capabilities to serve clients in New Jersey and beyond.

+ TOPSPIN CONSUMER PARTNERS CLOSES $328 MILLION FUND WITH LEGAL SUPPORT FROM GOODWIN PROCTER

Goodwin Procter LLP supported Topspin Consumer Partners in closing its latest fundraising effort, securing $328 million for a consumer-focused fund. The achievement marks a notable development in the private equity landscape, with Goodwin Procter providing essential counsel throughout the process.On April 15, 2026, Topspin announced the fund’s close, signaling an intent to capitalize on opportunities within the consumer sector. The firm’s legal team helped navigate complex fundraising dynamics, ensure regulatory compliance, and streamline commitments from potential investors.The fund will pursue a wide range of opportunities across consumer markets, with a focus on sectors showing robust growth. Through Goodwin Procter’s support, Topspin Consumer Partners seeks to identify and invest in companies well-positioned to perform in a changing market environment.The collaboration highlights Goodwin Procter’s reputation in private equity and its ongoing commitment to helping clients achieve their investment objectives, potentially boosting investor confidence as the fund scales.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK ADDS EMPLOYMENT LEADER TO CALIFORNIA TECH TEAM IN SACRAMENTO

Orrick has appointed Sarah Tauman as a partner in its California Employment practice, based in Sacramento, effective 12th May 2026. She will advise venture-backed technology companies on employment matters as they scale their operations and expand their businesses. Tauman brings experience across health tech, fintech and consumer tech, including guidance on employment considerations in mergers and acquisitions and initial public offerings. Her appointment fits into Orrick's broader strategy to expand its Tech Companies platform in 2026 and follows recent hires of Agatha Kluk in New York and Gina Marek in Silicon Valley. The California Employment practice is recognized as Band 1 by Chambers, reflecting its deep industry knowledge. Julie Totten, Leader of Global Employment Law and Litigation Practice, said the demand for employment counseling spans all growth stages, and Tauman added, 'Orrick is known for leadership in Tech and Employment, and particularly for innovation. I’m excited to be joining such a highly regarded team, and I look forward to collaborating to serve our clients.'

+ ORRICK ADDS EXPERIENCED USPTO VETERAN TO EXPAND IP PRACTICE

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has expanded its IP practice by hiring a senior attorney from the USPTO, a move that reflects the firm's ongoing push into technology and telecommunications sectors.The new attorney will officially join on the 28th of April, 2026, bringing a wealth of experience from the USPTO, including patent examination and litigation.The hire is expected to augment Orrick's ability to guide clients through patent strategy and protection in rapidly changing markets.Orrick's managing partner commented that the addition reinforces the firm's commitment to best-in-class IP services, with professionals who understand patent law's intricacies.Industry observers note that the firm's ability to attract talent from leading institutions could influence its growth trajectory and competitive position in technology-driven sectors.

+ NICOLE LOPEZ JOINS ORRICK TO DRIVE YOUTH ONLINE SAFETY STRATEGY AND REGULATION

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe announced the addition of Nicole Lopez, a recognized authority in youth online safety policy and litigation, to its team. Lopez previously held a senior role at Meta, underscoring her capability to navigate complex safety issues as the digital environment evolves.On Apr. 28, 2026, Lopez expressed enthusiasm for the new role and the opportunity to broaden her impact in the field.Her background spans policy, litigation and regulatory strategy, which the firm says will help clients facing increasing scrutiny over online safety. Jeremy Kudon, who leads Orrick's State Legislative practice, said, "We are facing a watershed moment. Online safety is top of mind for innovators, government, teens and parents, and society at large." The firm notes that 25 state legislatures are actively considering legislation aimed at safeguarding minors in digital spaces.Aravind Swaminathan, Orrick's Tech Sector Co-Leader, called Lopez a unique blend of skills—"part policy expert, part litigation and regulatory strategist, and part business advisor"—and said she will be an extraordinary resource for clients as AI reshapes the digital landscape. The Online Safety Law Center will leverage her leadership to keep clients informed of rapid developments and to advocate for policies that balance innovation with child protection.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JK4-CP13-RS50-G30T-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - CURRENT NEWS FROM THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - CURRENT NEWS FROM THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ PILLSBURY OPENS BOSTON OFFICE, EXPANDING NATIONAL FOOTPRINT

On 12th May 2026, Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman announced it would open an office in Boston, expanding its footprint in one of the United States' largest legal and business markets. The launch will commence with a team of five senior lawyers, including a litigator recruited from Latham & Watkins.Robert Gilbert, who joined Pillsbury after seven years at Latham, specializes in representing policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters. He has advised Boston-area clients such as Harvard University, UniFirst Corporation, and Northland Investment Corporation. Gilbert will help launch the Boston office alongside Pillsbury leaders Michael Zeliger, Andrew Troop, Don Lonczak, and David Simons.David Dekker, chair and CEO of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, underscored the strategic value of the Boston outpost, citing the city's dynamic startup ecosystem, leading research institutions, and a dense network of financial services and private capital. He said, "Pillsbury has long represented clients headquartered in the Boston area and those connected to the Boston-Cambridge innovation ecosystem. Opening a Boston office is a natural next step given our capabilities and relationships in the tech, life sciences, healthcare, and financial industries in Boston and around the world." This move marks Pillsbury’s 17th location in the United States and its 25th globally. The Boston launch places the firm among other major law firms expanding in the city, including Reed Smith, Freshfields, and Simpson Thacher & Bartlett, as Boston broadens its powerhouse mix of technology, life sciences, and finance. With the Boston office, Pillsbury aims to deepen engagement with local clients and industries, reinforcing its role in the evolving regional legal landscape.

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST-PERKINS COIE MERGER SECURES STRONG PARTNER BACKING

The merger of Ashurst and Perkins Coie into Ashurst Perkins Coie has won overwhelming support from the partnerships of both firms. The decision was confirmed after a consultation period, with the approval announced on 13th April 2026. The deal is expected to close in the third quarter of the year, subject to customary closing conditions.Perkins Coie's Mark Birnbaum said the approval reflects partners' commitment to creating a forward looking firm and a shared confidence in their ability to grow and deliver high quality legal services in a changing global market. Ashurst chief executive Paul Jenkins stressed the alignment between the two practices and that combining strengths will benefit clients.The merged firm will focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services. Perkins Coie firmwide managing partner Bill Malley expressed optimism that the combination will improve the firms capacity to handle complex cross border matters. The new entity will count roughly 3,000 lawyers across 52 offices in 32 countries, with major hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York.The merger first announced last year brings together two law firms with combined revenues of around USD 2.8 billion. Their lawyers have wide international experience, having worked in Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. The leadership of the new firm will be shared by Malley and Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Ashurst global chair Karen Davies and Perkins Coie corporate practice chair Brian Eiting will serve as co-chairs.

+ PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST SEAL MERGER TO CREATE ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, reflecting strong support for a union with complementary geographic reach, sector depth, and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will emerge as a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion. The new entity will count roughly 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, with flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. It will be noted for strengths in technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services sectors—industries pivotal to the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, said, 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform—one with the scale, sector depth, and technological leadership to meet our clients' increasingly cross-border needs.' Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, added, 'This vote confirms the strong alignment between our firms and our joint ambition for the future,' highlighting the complementary expertise across sectors and practice areas that will benefit clients. The merger will create a compelling platform for top legal talent.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026, positioning the new firm to navigate the complexities of the global market and deliver enhanced legal services.

Perkins Coie has opened a representative office in Shenzhen, marking its comeback in the Chinese market. The development also unfolds as several international firms push deeper into Shenzhen's Greater Bay Area, even as Perkins Coie weighs a potential merger with UK-based Ashurst that could create a larger global platform.

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER ANALYZES SENATE BAN ON PREDICTION MARKET TRADING

The Senate unanimously approved a resolution on 29th April 2026 banning Senators and their staff from participating in prediction markets. Morrison & Foerster notes that the move is part of a broader effort to regulate these markets, which have drawn both enthusiasm and skepticism.Hours after the resolution, Senators Kristen Gillibrand (D-NY) and Dave McCormick (R-PA) unveiled legislation to bar government officials from trading on prediction markets. The proposed bill would set standards for insider trading, introduce anti-money-laundering measures, and create an independent Office of the Retail Advocate within the CFTC, signaling a tighter regulatory environment. In parallel, Kalshi announced features intended to restrict underage access, including mandatory face ID and two-factor authentication for parents, aligning with the proposed framework and aiming to bolster consumer trust.A Senate hearing on prediction markets and sports betting is scheduled for 20th May 2026, with Former Representative Patrick McHenry slated to testify alongside industry representatives. CFTC Chairman Michael Selig has defended the commission’s exclusive jurisdiction in a Wall Street Journal letter, describing them as innovative financial products rather than gambling sites, a stance Morrison & Foerster says is critical to sustaining legitimacy amid heightened scrutiny.

urn:contentItem:6JK7-JTR3-RSBB-S10G-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ PILLSBURY ANNOUNCES BOSTON OFFICE AS PART OF GLOBAL EXPANSION

On 12th May 2026, Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman announced plans to open an office in Boston, expanding its presence in a major U.S. legal market with a team of five senior lawyers, including a litigator hired from Latham & Watkins.Robert Gilbert, who arrives from Latham after seven years, focuses on policyholder insurance recovery and brings experience with Harvard University, UniFirst Corporation, and Northland Investment Corporation. He will help establish the Boston office alongside Michael Zeliger, Andrew Troop, Don Lonczak, and David Simons.Chair and CEO David Dekker said the Boston outpost aligns with the city’s startup and research strengths, noting Pillsbury’s long history of serving Boston-area clients. He added, "Pillsbury has long represented clients headquartered in the Boston area and those connected to the Boston-Cambridge innovation ecosystem. Opening a Boston office is a natural next step given our capabilities and relationships in the tech, life sciences, healthcare, and financial industries in Boston and around the world." This expansion marks Pillsbury’s 17th U.S. location and 25th globally, with Boston joining a wave of firms increasing their Boston presence.The move underscores Pillsbury’s intent to deepen ties with local clients and industries while leveraging its global platform in technology, life sciences, healthcare, and finance.

+ MORGAN LEWIS TAPS PILLSBURY TALENT TO DEEPEN BENEFITS PRACTICE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has added a prominent Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP attorney to its Employee Benefits team, reflecting a strategic response to rising complexity in benefits law.The attorney's background encompasses compliance and regulatory issues across employee benefits, with expectations to strengthen the firm's ability to provide comprehensive legal services.Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire supports Morgan Lewis' ongoing plan to expand its benefits practice in a changing regulatory environment, while Pillsbury has seen departures to other firms.The move underscores the competitive talent market in corporate law and signals Morgan Lewis' commitment to maintaining state-of-the-art capabilities for its diverse client base.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST AND PERKINS COIE LAUNCH GLOBAL FIRM ASHURST PERKINS COIE TO EXPAND CROSS-BORDER OFFERINGS

On 13th April 2026 the partnership vote to merge Ashurst and Perkins Coie into Ashurst Perkins Coie was approved, with a closing target in the third quarter subject to customary conditions.The leadership structure will feature Bill Malley and Paul Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Karen Davies of Ashurst and Brian Eiting of Perkins Coie will serve as co-chairs.The merged group will comprise about 3,000 lawyers in 52 offices across 32 countries, with core hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York and a focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services.The combined revenues are around USD 2.8 billion, reflecting extensive cross-border experience across cities such as Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. The aim is to sharpen competitive edge and position the firm for ongoing growth in the global market.

+ GLOBAL LAW FIRMS PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST UNITE UNDER ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, reflecting strong support for a union that combines geographic reach with sector depth and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will become a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion and about 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, including flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. The combined firm will be noted for its technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services capabilities, aligned with the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, remarked that the partnership's approval signals a belief that the merger will deliver a differentiated global platform with scale and leadership in technology to meet cross-border client needs. 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform.'Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, echoed the sentiment, emphasizing alignment between the firms and their joint future ambitions. 'This vote confirms our strong alignment and the compelling platform it creates for clients and talent alike,' he said. The parties noted that the merger will enhance cross-border service capability across key sectors.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026.

+ PERKINS COIE REENTERS CHINA MARKET THROUGH SHENZHEN OFFICE

Perkins Coie has opened a Shenzhen representative office, signaling a renewed foothold in China. The office was inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026. The move follows the 2024 closure of the firm's Beijing and Shanghai offices and reflects a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to leverage the city's growing tech and manufacturing base in the Greater Bay Area. Bing Ai and Wei Yuan will lead the office as chief representative and managing partner and as representative and litigation partner, respectively; both are US-trained and bring deep experience advising technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management for semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, says the Shenzhen launch reinforces the firm's cross-border US legal capabilities and its aim to help clients navigate global markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY GRABS $60 MILLION SERIES B BACKING

Crosby has raised $60 million in a Series B, a mark of its rapid fundraising that was announced on April 3, 2026.The round was led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors, Bain Capital Ventures and angel investor Elad Gil, with Cooley LLP listed among the backers.Funds will be used to expand Crosby’s technology and support a growing client base that includes Clay, Rogo and Ramp.The company blends artificial intelligence with legal know-how to speed contract reviews and says the total value of its negotiated contracts now exceeds $1 billion, up from about $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year. In a blog post, Crosby wrote, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The firm has introduced simulations of counterparty responses to redlines, voice agents that negotiate for clients, and a dashboard for clients to oversee Crosby’s work. Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, with Daniels a former Cooley associate and Sarihan a former Google software engineer and Ramp tech lead.

+ COOLEY WELCOMES FRESHFIELDS VETERANS TO BOLSTER TECHNOLOGY GROUP

Cooley LLP announced a substantial extension of its technology practice through the addition of several attorneys formerly with Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. The move is designed to enhance Cooley's tech capabilities and underscores the firm's aim to remain competitive amid a dynamic legal environment.As of 23rd November 2025, the recruitment drive underscores the firm's continued push to expand technology offerings in a rapidly evolving sector. The infusion of Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is expected to strengthen the existing practice and draw new clients needing technology-focused legal counsel.The incoming team members come with extensive experience advising leading clients on IP, data protection, and corporate tech transactions. Their expertise is expected to augment Cooley's well-established venture capital and startup advisory services, further cementing the firm’s leadership in technology law.Cooley's decision to recruit from Freshfields reflects a broader market trend in the legal industry: firms actively pursue top talent to meet demands of a fast-moving, tech-centric market. As tech-related legal challenges grow more complex, the need for specialized knowledge becomes critical, making these hires timely.With this growth, Cooley aims to solidify its status as a preferred destination for technology clients, reinforcing its capabilities in an area essential for innovation and growth. As integration proceeds, observers will monitor the impact on Cooley's market position and client services in the coming months.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ FOUNDER COMPLETES £11 MILLION SOFTWARE COMPANY ACQUISITION WITH REED SMITH COUNSEL

Reed Smith LLP supported an £11 million software company acquisition by its founder, with completion announced on 8th of May 2026, reflecting the firm's strength in navigating complex tech transactions.The founder aims to enhance operations and pursue growth, with Reed Smith delivering regulatory compliance guidance and aiding negotiations to close the deal.Analysts say the move aligns with a broader trend of strategic software acquisitions, and Reed Smith's advisory role is likely to attract investor confidence as the business enters a new chapter.

+ REED SMITH ADDS EXPERIENCED REAL ESTATE FINANCE LAWYER FROM STINSON TO DC OFFICE

ReedSmith LLP has hired a real estate finance attorney from Stinson LLP for its Washington, D.C. office to bolster its real estate and finance practice. The attorney's background includes financing, acquisition and development on significant projects, complementing Reed Smith's existing offerings.The move comes as the DC real estate market remains active and regulatory scrutiny grows, a context the firm says the new hire can help navigate.As of 7th May 2026, the firm emphasizes its commitment to growth and client service in real estate and finance.

+ REED SMITH BOLSTERS PRIVATE EQUITY PRACTICE IN LOS ANGELES WITH NEW PARTNER

Reed Smith has announced the addition of a private equity partner in its Los Angeles office, strengthening its capability in a market that remains pivotal for private capital flows.The new partner joins on 4th May 2026 and brings deep experience in private equity transactions and related advisory services, aligning with the firm's plan to broaden its private equity practice and serve investment firms and corporate clients involved in M&A.The appointment underscores Reed Smith's aim to provide strategic insights and tailored solutions as the private equity landscape evolves, supporting sophisticated investment structures and regulatory considerations.This strategic hire positions Reed Smith to pursue growth opportunities in private equity and continue delivering high-quality service and durable client relationships.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI P.C. [3 News That Matters]

+ SPACE BIOTECHNOLOGY STARTUP BIOORBIT CLOSES $13.2M SEED ROUND; WSGR HELPS GUIDE FINANCING

BioOrbit has announced a seed round of $13.2 million, co-led by LocalGlobe and BREEGA, with contributions from Seedcamp, Auxxo Female Catalyst Fund, Type One Ventures, 7percent Ventures and others. The financing was announced on 30th April 2026.BioOrbit aims to establish a space-based pharmaceuticals factory within the next decade and is developing hardware to enable large-scale crystallization of protein drugs, a core step in biopharmaceutical manufacturing.The company is led by Cambridge scientist Dr. Katie King and oncology researcher Dr. Leonor Teles, who are spearheading this space-focused platform.Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. provided corporate governance and emerging-company counsel to BioOrbit during the financing, with the team including Daniel Glazer, Jose Campos, Rashad Mohammed and Sophia Kunthara.The deal underscores rising investor interest in space-enabled life sciences, and BioOrbit will share updates on LinkedIn and in UKTN coverage as plans progress.

+ WILSON SONSINI ELEVATES ENERGY TRANSITION CAPABILITIES WITH KEN SANOCKI APPOINTMENT

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati has added Ken Sanocki as a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions practice, aimed at expanding the firm's ability to help clients finance energy infrastructure through the transition to cleaner power.Sanocki comes from Sunrun, where as vice president of legal he oversaw all project-finance matters, including tax equity financings and complex debt structures for distributed-energy projects. He led more than 50 tax equity financings totaling over $10 billion.Doug Clark, the firm's managing partner, said that Sanocki has operated at the center of the energy transition and will bring deep knowledge of tax equity and structured financings to clients navigating energy financing.In his new role, Sanocki will counsel on structured and project finance across renewable energy, storage, and climate infrastructure, assist with capital formation and strategic transactions, and help clients develop revenue-generating asset portfolios and prepare for IPOs or M&A. He began his career at Milbank after serving as SunEdison senior counsel for global structured and project finance; he earned a J.D. from NYU and a B.A. from Boston College.

+ WILSON SONSINI SUPPORTS OFF PISTE CAPITAL IN LANDMARK FUND DEBUT BACKED BY EDITION VENTURES

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. advised Off Piste Capital in forming its inaugural fund, Off Piste Fund I, which closed on 29th April 2026 with backing from strategic limited partners including Edition Ventures.The fund targets investments in companies building the systems and infrastructure of the future. Off Piste has already invested in four startups: Stendr, a Norway-based firm delivering AI-driven counter-drone technology; Objection, a UK startup creating accountability infrastructure for public information; Beyond Reach Labs, a US YC-backed venture developing deployable space structures; and Octet, a UK company specializing in cryptographic proofs of physical location.The deal team at Wilson Sonsini included Bradley Doline, Alice Cao, and Stephanie Yasay, whose work on capital markets and fund formation helped navigate the complexities of launching a VC fund. This collaboration underscores the firm's ongoing support for emerging venture activity and its expanding footprint in the tech investment landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN HELPS CLOSE $1.3 BILLION MULTIFAMILY-FOCUSED S3 VEHICLE

Sidley Austin has advised on the closure of a $1.3 billion multifamily-focused S3 fund, a milestone for the real estate investment sector. The announcement, dated 7th May 2026, highlights increasing demand for multifamily housing driven by demographic shifts and urbanization. The S3 vehicle targets multifamily properties and is positioned to attract investor interest as housing needs evolve. Urban density and solid rental fundamentals underpin the appeal of such assets, with Sidley guiding the fund's formation and regulatory compliance. Over recent years, Sidley has built a track record of delivering legal counsel tailored to real estate investments, assisting clients through fund formation and regulatory requirements. The successful close reinforces the firm's market standing as it continues to advise on high-value transactions. Looking ahead, Sidley remains focused on delivering comprehensive legal solutions as the market evolves, with this closing serving as evidence of its capacity to facilitate substantial real estate investments.

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN GUIDES TELIX IN 50:50 CO-DEVELOPMENT AND CO-COMMERCIALISATION DEAL WITH REGENERON WORTH USD 2.1 BILLION

Telix Pharmaceuticals has enlisted Sidley Austin to guide its collaboration with Regeneron, a deal valued at up to USD 2.1 billion that covers the co-development and co-commercialisation of next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies. The arrangement provides for a 50:50 split of costs and profits and includes an upfront USD 40 million payment for four initial programmes. Announced on 21st April 2026, the agreement is built to pool the companies' strengths in oncology and radiopharmaceuticals, aiming to move candidates through development more efficiently while sharing regulatory and governance responsibilities. Sidley partner Joshua Hofheimer noted that governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property are central to these collaborations, stating that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The alliance reflects a broader push in the biopharma space toward strategic collaborations designed to accelerate innovation in cancer care, a trend that law firms like Sidley are well-placed to support given the increasing complexity of cross-border deals.

+ RECRUITMENT PUSH DRIVES 33% GROWTH IN SIDLEY AUSTIN'S CITY OPERATIONS

Sidley Austin reported a 33% rise in City revenue, attributed to its hiring strategy as of 14th April 2026. Management credits a focused approach to identifying and integrating skilled professionals that expanded the City teams and diversified practice areas. The improvements in service have helped lift billable hours and client retention, reinforcing confidence in the hiring model. Analysts say the move demonstrates a willingness to invest in human capital to keep pace with a shifting legal market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ LONDON OFFICE GROWS TECH M&A CAPABILITIES AS THREE PARTNERS JOIN MORRISON FOERSTER

Morrison Foerster says it has expanded its tech transactions team with three London-based partners: Mike Pierides, Will Holder, and Oliver Bell, adding to its global technology transactions and AI-focused M&A platform. The trio brings more than 55 years of combined experience. The firm announced this on the 9th of May, 2026.Pierides concentrates on technology and commercial transactions, including outsourcing, data privacy and cybersecurity, GPU-as-a-service agreements, licensing, and technology-as-a-service mandates, serving clients in AI, data centers, fintech, aviation, telecommunications, and financial services.Holder broadens Morrison Foerster's tech and media M&A practice across public and private M&A, private equity, joint ventures, and corporate matters, with work spanning technology, music, sports, media, fintech, and consumer sectors. Bell is set to join in the coming weeks with experience in technology-led transactions, large-scale IT outsourcing deals, and cybersecurity.The hires are intended to strengthen cross-border technology transactions and AI-related capabilities and align with Morrison Foerster's London growth strategy. They mark the eighth, ninth, and tenth partner additions in the firm's London office in the past year, reflecting rising demand for AI, fintech, data centers, and digital transformation. Eric McCrath, Chair, said the firm's investment in core tech M&A capabilities remains central, while London partner Andrew Boyd highlighted the synergies with the practice areas.

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER REVIEWS SENATE ACTION RESTRICTING PREDICTION MARKET PARTICIPATION

The Senate moved on 29th April 2026 to ban Senators and their staff from trading in prediction markets, a resolution Morrison & Foerster says reflects growing regulatory focus on these platforms.Hours later, Gillibrand and McCormick introduced legislation to bar government officials from prediction market trading, proposing insider trading standards, AML measures, and an independent Retail Advocate within the CFTC.Kalshi announced underage- restriction updates, including face ID and parent-based two-factor authentication, aligning with the proposed framework and aiming to boost consumer trust.A Senate hearing on prediction markets and sports betting is planned for 20th May 2026, with Patrick McHenry testifying alongside industry representatives; Selig defended CFTC jurisdiction in a Wall Street Journal letter, stressing the markets as innovative financial products.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ FIRST AI UNICORN IN LATIN AMERICA EMERGES AS ENTER SEALS $100 MILLION SERIES B

SAO PAULO, May 8, 2026 — Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented Enter, a Brazil-based AI legal-technology company, in its Series B that raised over $100 million and valued Enter at about $1.2 billion, marking Enter as Latin America’s first AI unicorn. The round was led by Founders Fund with significant participation from Sequoia Capital, Ribbit Capital, Kaszek, Atlantico and ONEVC. Enter, founded in 2023, serves major clients including Airbnb, Nubank, Mercado Libre and Latam Airlines, among more than 40 partners. Enter uses AI to automate key litigation tasks, from evidence discovery to drafting responses and negotiating settlements, with human review by Enter’s legal network. Greenberg Traurig’s deal team was led by Matthew Squires and included Maurice Guttmann, L. Frank Cordero, Agata Jacqueline Paramesvari and Nathalya Zara, underscoring the firm’s cross-border capabilities across 51 offices and a 3,100-lawyer platform.

+ GERARD C. MARTIN JOINS GREENBERG TRAURIG AS CHICAGO SHAREHOLDER IN RESTRUCTURING PRACTICE

On 7th May 2026, Greenberg Traurig, LLP announced the expansion of its Chicago office with Gerard C. Martin joining as a shareholder in the Restructuring & Special Situations Practice, coming from Reed Smith LLP. His practice includes cash-flow and asset-based lending, leveraged finance, acquisition financings, intercreditor arrangements, and equipment and aviation finance, along with project finance matters. He also handles financial restructurings, advising secured creditors and debtors in in-court and out-of-court matters, including debtor-in-possession financings and distressed investing. His industry experience spans manufacturing, energy, aviation, technology, health care and financial services, expanding Greenberg Traurig's cross-sector capabilities. Martin said the firm’s platform aligns with his work, adding that Greenberg Traurig is an ideal fit, and Shari L. Heyen and Oscar N. Pinkas noted the Chicago office will leverage his expertise.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG NAVIGATES SECOND JAPANESE HYPERSCALE DEAL FOR CLIENT, HIGHLIGHTING DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE EXPERTISE

Greenberg Traurig, LLP advised the seller in the sale of a stabilized 40-megawatt hyperscale data center in Keihanna, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan, near Osaka. The closing occurred on 7th May 2026 and is among the largest asset-level sales of a fully leased hyperscale facility in Japan. The buyer is a CapitaLand Ascendas REIT-led consortium, purchasing a 49% stake with the remaining 51% going to a fund managed by an affiliate of a Japanese trading company. The seller was a joint venture between a US institutional manager’s fund and a fund sponsored by a Japanese trading company; the center is leased to a single US hyperscale cloud provider. This transaction marks the second Japanese hyperscale data center sale for this client, following a deal completed in November. Greenberg Traurig’s Digital Infrastructure Industry Group, with over 110 attorneys worldwide, supports clients across data center and telecom transactions.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ESPERION'S $1.1B PRIVATISATION SIGNALS PRIVATE-EQUITY PULL IN BIOTECH, UNDER LEGAL HELM OF SIDLEY AND GOODWIN

Esperion Therapeutics Inc. has agreed to a $1.1 billion take-private, with Sidley Austin LLP and Goodwin Procter LLP leading the legal work. The announcement, made on 1st May 2026, positions the lipid-management specialist to focus on its programs outside the public markets.Private ownership is gaining traction in biotech as firms seek to streamline R&D and accelerate commercialization.Sidley and Goodwin's involvement reflects their strength in complex M&A deals and signals rigorous governance as Esperion transitions to a private structure.Analysts note that the move could enable longer-term strategic planning and potentially faster growth, while investors monitor the company's performance in its new privately held state.

+ FBT GIBBONS TAPS GOODWIN PROCTER VETERAN TO STRENGTHEN VC CAPABILITIES

FBT Gibbons is expanding its capabilities in venture capital by hiring from Goodwin Procter LLP for its New Jersey office. On 21st April 2026, the firm confirmed the recruitment of a venture capital attorney. The attorney comes with extensive venture capital experience from Goodwin Procter LLP, a leader in the field, and the firm plans to leverage that expertise for clients seeking guidance on capital raises. Industry observers note the hire reflects a broader trend of law firms sharpening niche practices to compete in a growing market.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER ENABLES CLOSURE OF $328 MILLION CONSUMER-FOCUSED FUND FOR TOPSPIN

Goodwin Procter LLP helped close Topspin Consumer Partners' latest fundraising, securing a $328 million commitment for a consumer-focused fund. The deal underscores a growing trend in private equity toward consumer-oriented strategies, with Goodwin Procter providing crucial legal support throughout the process.On April 15, 2026, Topspin announced the fund’s closure, designed to pursue opportunities in the consumer sector. The firm’s counsel was instrumental in navigating fundraising requirements, maintaining regulatory compliance, and simplifying investor workflows.The fund intends to target a broad spectrum of opportunities across consumer industries, emphasizing segments with strong growth trajectories. With Goodwin Procter’s guidance, Topspin Consumer Partners aims to back innovative ventures well positioned to compete in a dynamic market.This partnership reinforces Goodwin Procter’s leadership in private equity work and its dedication to client success, with potential implications for investor confidence as the fund progresses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK TAPS EMPLOYMENT EXPERT TO BOLSTER CALIFORNIA TECH PLATFORM

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has named Sarah Tauman as a partner in its California Employment team based in Sacramento, effective 12th May 2026. Tauman will advise venture-backed technology companies on employment matters as they scale. Her practice spans health tech, fintech and consumer tech, including guidance on employment issues in mergers and acquisitions and initial public offerings, supporting growth trajectories for tech firms. This marks the third key addition to Orrick's Tech Companies platform in 2026, following Agatha Kluk in New York and Gina Marek in Silicon Valley. The California Employment practice is ranked Band 1 by Chambers. Totten noted the ongoing demand for employment counseling across all growth stages, and Tauman said, 'Orrick is known for leadership in Tech and Employment, and particularly for innovation.'

+ ORRICK WELCOMES SENIOR USPTO LAWYER AS IP PRACTICE GROWS

Orrick has expanded its IP practice by hiring a senior attorney from the USPTO, aiming to enhance capabilities in patent matters as the firm grows in tech and telecom.The new attorney will join on the 28th of April, 2026, and brings extensive patent examination and litigation experience.Orrick's leadership described the hire as evidence of the firm's commitment to top-tier IP services and to navigating the evolving IP rights landscape in the digital age.With demand for technology-focused counsel rising, the firm expects this addition to support clients facing complex patent challenges and strengthen its competitive standing.

+ FORMER META YOUTH SAFETY POLICY LEAD JOINS ORRICK

On Apr. 23, 2026, Orrick announced the addition of the former Meta youth safety policy head to its team, a move designed to expand its digital safety and compliance capabilities.The new hire comes with a strong track record in policy design and implementation, particularly around privacy and the protection of young users online.Orrick frames the appointment as part of its proactive response to evolving regulatory requirements governing youth online safety.The firm expects the hire to strengthen its advisory services as tech companies face heightened scrutiny and regulatory complexity.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
