+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. [4 Press Releases]

+ PETER LANDAU JOINS LAW FIRM BABST CALLAND

Peter J. Landau recently joined law firm Babst Calland's Pittsburgh, Pa. office as an associate in the Corporate and Commercial and Emerging Technologies groups. He advises clients across a variety of industries on a broad range of corporate and commercial legal matters, including mergers and acquisitions, commercial contracts, corporate structuring and governance, and general corporate and business counseling.

Before joining the Firm, Mr. Landau was an associate with DLA Piper in Palo Alto, Calif., where he advised U.S.- and foreign-based companies on domestic and international tax matters, including international, M&A, state and local, and renewable energy tax credits. He is a 2022 graduate of Columbia Law School.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. ANNA SKIPPER JEWART SELECTED BY THE LEGAL INTELLIGENCER AS A "2026 LAWYER ON THE FAST TRACK, SMALL/MIDSIZE FIRMS WINNER"

Babst Calland Attorney Anna Skipper Jewart was selected by The Legal Intelligencer as a "2026 Lawyer on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winner" in Pennsylvania.

After reviewing the results of nominations for the annual The Legal Intelligencer awards, a judging panel composed of evaluators from all corners of the legal profession and the state selected 11 attorneys as the 2026 Lawyers on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winners. This recognition is only given to attorneys under the age of 40 who have demonstrated excellence in four categories: development of the law; advocacy and community contributions; service to the bar; and peer and public recognition.

Ms. Jewart is an associate in the Firm's Public Sector, Real Estate, Land Use and Zoning, Energy and Natural Resources, and Data Center Development groups. Her practice focuses on municipal and land use law in both the private and public sectors. Ms. Jewart assists private clients in navigating zoning and other land use processes across Pennsylvania. In particular, she specializes in renewable energy development, data center development, and similar industries. In addition, Ms. Jewart represents public clients in municipal solicitorship and special counsel roles. She is a 2019 graduate, cum laude, Boston College of Law.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. DON BLUEDORN AND ETHAN JOHNSON DISCUSS GENAI PRODUCTS FOR USE IN LEGAL PRACTICE AT ACC DRAFT-ING DAY

Attorneys Don Bluedorn and Ethan Johnson presented "The AI Playbook - Strategizing Artificial Intelligence in Legal Practice" at the Western Pennsylvania Chapter of the Association of Corporate Counsel's (ACC) Draft-ing Day.

Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping the legal profession, much like how NFL teams draft, strategize, and execute on the field. This CLE program provided attorneys and in-house counsel with an "AI playbook" designed to help understand, implement, and manage artificial intelligence technologies while maintaining compliance, ethical integrity, and risk mitigation.

Don and Ethan discussed GenAI products for use in the legal practice, focusing on available products, how the products can be most effectively used in the legal practice, and best practices to avoid GenAI-related pitfalls (both for lawyers using GenAI and for lawyers supervising others who are using GenAI).

Source: Company Website

+ THERESA DAVIS JOINS BABST CALLAND'S HARRISBURG OFFICE

Theresa Davis recently joined Babst Calland's Harrisburg, Pa. office as an associate in the Employment and Labor and Public Sector groups. Ms. Davis represents public and private employers in a wide range of labor and employment matters. Her practice includes all aspects of state and federal litigation involving employment claims, as well as advising clients on day-to-day workplace issues and compliance strategies. She also represents municipal clients on a wide range of matters.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: WEEKLY CORPORATE REVIEW: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHS-XX63-RTHK-1270-00000-00WEEKLY CORPORATE REVIEW: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisWEEKLY CORPORATE REVIEW: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. [4 Press Releases]

+ PETER LANDAU JOINS LAW FIRM BABST CALLAND

Peter J. Landau recently joined law firm Babst Calland's Pittsburgh, Pa. office as an associate in the Corporate and Commercial and Emerging Technologies groups. He advises clients across a variety of industries on a broad range of corporate and commercial legal matters, including mergers and acquisitions, commercial contracts, corporate structuring and governance, and general corporate and business counseling.

Before joining the Firm, Mr. Landau was an associate with DLA Piper in Palo Alto, Calif., where he advised U.S.- and foreign-based companies on domestic and international tax matters, including international, M&A, state and local, and renewable energy tax credits. He is a 2022 graduate of Columbia Law School.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. ANNA SKIPPER JEWART SELECTED BY THE LEGAL INTELLIGENCER AS A "2026 LAWYER ON THE FAST TRACK, SMALL/MIDSIZE FIRMS WINNER"

Babst Calland Attorney Anna Skipper Jewart was selected by The Legal Intelligencer as a "2026 Lawyer on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winner" in Pennsylvania.

After reviewing the results of nominations for the annual The Legal Intelligencer awards, a judging panel composed of evaluators from all corners of the legal profession and the state selected 11 attorneys as the 2026 Lawyers on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winners. This recognition is only given to attorneys under the age of 40 who have demonstrated excellence in four categories: development of the law; advocacy and community contributions; service to the bar; and peer and public recognition.

Ms. Jewart is an associate in the Firm's Public Sector, Real Estate, Land Use and Zoning, Energy and Natural Resources, and Data Center Development groups. Her practice focuses on municipal and land use law in both the private and public sectors. Ms. Jewart assists private clients in navigating zoning and other land use processes across Pennsylvania. In particular, she specializes in renewable energy development, data center development, and similar industries. In addition, Ms. Jewart represents public clients in municipal solicitorship and special counsel roles. She is a 2019 graduate, cum laude, Boston College of Law.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. DON BLUEDORN AND ETHAN JOHNSON DISCUSS GENAI PRODUCTS FOR USE IN LEGAL PRACTICE AT ACC DRAFT-ING DAY

Attorneys Don Bluedorn and Ethan Johnson presented "The AI Playbook - Strategizing Artificial Intelligence in Legal Practice" at the Western Pennsylvania Chapter of the Association of Corporate Counsel's (ACC) Draft-ing Day.

Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping the legal profession, much like how NFL teams draft, strategize, and execute on the field. This CLE program provided attorneys and in-house counsel with an "AI playbook" designed to help understand, implement, and manage artificial intelligence technologies while maintaining compliance, ethical integrity, and risk mitigation.

Don and Ethan discussed GenAI products for use in the legal practice, focusing on available products, how the products can be most effectively used in the legal practice, and best practices to avoid GenAI-related pitfalls (both for lawyers using GenAI and for lawyers supervising others who are using GenAI).

Source: Company Website

+ THERESA DAVIS JOINS BABST CALLAND'S HARRISBURG OFFICE

Theresa Davis recently joined Babst Calland's Harrisburg, Pa. office as an associate in the Employment and Labor and Public Sector groups. Ms. Davis represents public and private employers in a wide range of labor and employment matters. Her practice includes all aspects of state and federal litigation involving employment claims, as well as advising clients on day-to-day workplace issues and compliance strategies. She also represents municipal clients on a wide range of matters.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: WEEKLY CORPORATE COVERAGE: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-0823-S5JV-R22G-00000-00WEEKLY CORPORATE COVERAGE: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisWEEKLY CORPORATE COVERAGE: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. [4 Press Releases]

+ PETER LANDAU JOINS LAW FIRM BABST CALLAND

Peter J. Landau recently joined law firm Babst Calland's Pittsburgh, Pa. office as an associate in the Corporate and Commercial and Emerging Technologies groups. He advises clients across a variety of industries on a broad range of corporate and commercial legal matters, including mergers and acquisitions, commercial contracts, corporate structuring and governance, and general corporate and business counseling.

Before joining the Firm, Mr. Landau was an associate with DLA Piper in Palo Alto, Calif., where he advised U.S.- and foreign-based companies on domestic and international tax matters, including international, M&A, state and local, and renewable energy tax credits. He is a 2022 graduate of Columbia Law School.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. ANNA SKIPPER JEWART SELECTED BY THE LEGAL INTELLIGENCER AS A "2026 LAWYER ON THE FAST TRACK, SMALL/MIDSIZE FIRMS WINNER"

Babst Calland Attorney Anna Skipper Jewart was selected by The Legal Intelligencer as a "2026 Lawyer on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winner" in Pennsylvania.

After reviewing the results of nominations for the annual The Legal Intelligencer awards, a judging panel composed of evaluators from all corners of the legal profession and the state selected 11 attorneys as the 2026 Lawyers on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winners. This recognition is only given to attorneys under the age of 40 who have demonstrated excellence in four categories: development of the law; advocacy and community contributions; service to the bar; and peer and public recognition.

Ms. Jewart is an associate in the Firm's Public Sector, Real Estate, Land Use and Zoning, Energy and Natural Resources, and Data Center Development groups. Her practice focuses on municipal and land use law in both the private and public sectors. Ms. Jewart assists private clients in navigating zoning and other land use processes across Pennsylvania. In particular, she specializes in renewable energy development, data center development, and similar industries. In addition, Ms. Jewart represents public clients in municipal solicitorship and special counsel roles. She is a 2019 graduate, cum laude, Boston College of Law.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. DON BLUEDORN AND ETHAN JOHNSON DISCUSS GENAI PRODUCTS FOR USE IN LEGAL PRACTICE AT ACC DRAFT-ING DAY

Attorneys Don Bluedorn and Ethan Johnson presented "The AI Playbook - Strategizing Artificial Intelligence in Legal Practice" at the Western Pennsylvania Chapter of the Association of Corporate Counsel's (ACC) Draft-ing Day.

Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping the legal profession, much like how NFL teams draft, strategize, and execute on the field. This CLE program provided attorneys and in-house counsel with an "AI playbook" designed to help understand, implement, and manage artificial intelligence technologies while maintaining compliance, ethical integrity, and risk mitigation.

Don and Ethan discussed GenAI products for use in the legal practice, focusing on available products, how the products can be most effectively used in the legal practice, and best practices to avoid GenAI-related pitfalls (both for lawyers using GenAI and for lawyers supervising others who are using GenAI).

Source: Company Website

+ THERESA DAVIS JOINS BABST CALLAND'S HARRISBURG OFFICE

Theresa Davis recently joined Babst Calland's Harrisburg, Pa. office as an associate in the Employment and Labor and Public Sector groups. Ms. Davis represents public and private employers in a wide range of labor and employment matters. Her practice includes all aspects of state and federal litigation involving employment claims, as well as advising clients on day-to-day workplace issues and compliance strategies. She also represents municipal clients on a wide range of matters.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: May 04, 2026: HINES INTERESTS LP AND PEERS INDUSTRY NEWS COMPILATION

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH9-NGK3-SFD6-333B-00000-00May 04, 2026: HINES INTERESTS LP AND PEERS INDUSTRY NEWS COMPILATION2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 04, 2026: HINES INTERESTS LP AND PEERS INDUSTRY NEWS COMPILATION

On 22nd April 2026, Hines Interests LP global head of real estate, Steve Luthman, articulated the firm's strategic emphasis on asset management amidst fluctuating interest rates and a pricing reset in the real estate sector. The company, operating in 30 countries, is adapting to market conditions by enhancing tenant relationships and operational efficiencies to sustain performance across its portfolio. Luthman detailed the firm's philosophy, emphasizing the necessity for adaptability and strategic foresight in a rapidly changing market environment.

Expanding its European footprint, Hines Interests LP announced a significant lease agreement on 1st April 2026 with NXP Semiconductors Germany. The technology firm secured 10,000 square meters at the modern Aer building in Munich, reflecting the ongoing demand for premium office spaces in regional innovation hubs and aligning with the firm's vision of fostering innovation-driven environments.

In related real estate developments, Boston Properties, Inc. reported strong leasing momentum in San Francisco's South Financial District on 18th April 2026. The firm achieved full occupancy at its 50 Hawthorne property, with Dropbox leasing the entire 64,000-square-foot building, and surpassed 90% occupancy at the neighboring 680 Folsom property following commitments from Decagon and Swinerton Builders.

Furthermore, Boston Properties completed a landmark transaction on 4th March 2026 involving the formal transfer of digital rights alongside physical property. The $132 million sale of the 140 Kendrick Street suburban office campus to Cross Ocean Partners and Lincoln Property Company marked an industry first. Bryan Koop, Executive Vice President of the firm's Boston region, stated that the transaction reflects a broader shift in how leading institutions approach real estate in a digital world.

Shifting to corporate finance activity within the sector, CBRE Group Inc. successfully priced a $750 million offering of 5.250% senior notes due in 2036. Announced on 28th April 2026, the notes were issued at 98.947% of face value. The company expects approximately $737 million in net proceeds, which will primarily be used to repay borrowings under its existing commercial paper program to enhance liquidity.

On the personnel front, CBRE Group reinforced its Greater Boston operations on 10th April 2026 by appointing Shay Kenney and Brooke Howard as Vice Presidents. Kenney joins the Office Leasing division focusing on landlord representation, while Howard enters the Capital Markets team specializing in investment sales across multiple asset classes.

In the investment management space, Blackstone Inc. announced a €2 billion investment in Eurowind Energy on 29th April 2026 to acquire a 24.7% stake. Gert Vinther Jørgensen, chairman of the board of Eurowind Energy, noted that with Blackstone as a long-term investor, the company gains the financial strength required to enter its next development phase. Adam Kuhnley, co-head of European investments at Blackstone infrastructure, highlighted the strong business built by the management team across the renewable energy value chain.

Just a day prior, on 28th April 2026, Blackstone Inc. declared the final close of its Blackstone Life Sciences VI fund, raising a record $6.3 billion. Julian Ashworth, a partner at the Maples Group which advised on the transaction, emphasized that the successful fundraising underlines the investment firm's strategic position and ongoing commitment to advancing innovation in the life sciences sector.

value.Title: Wilson Sonsini Adds Project Finance Leader Ken Sanocki to Expand Energy and Climate Solutions Practice Former Sunrun Executive Led More Than $10 Billion in Tax Equity Financings Across Distributed Energy Platforms

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-NY83-SG4T-N0NX-00000-00Wilson Sonsini Adds Project Finance Leader Ken Sanocki to Expand Energy and Climate Solutions Practice2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:57ZLexisNexisWilson Sonsini Adds Project Finance Leader Ken Sanocki to Expand Energy and Climate Solutions PracticeFormer Sunrun Executive Led More Than $10 Billion in Tax Equity Financings Across Distributed Energy PlatformsSAN FRANCISCO

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati, the premier provider of legal services to technology, life sciences, and growth enterprises worldwide, today announced that Ken Sanocki has joined the firm as a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions (ECS) practice in San Francisco. His addition enhances the firm's ability to advise clients across the full lifecycle of energy infrastructure financing-from early-stage development through complex capital markets transactions.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260505836362/en/

Ken Sanocki, Partner, Wilson Sonsini

Sanocki joins from Sunrun, the nation's leading residential solar and storage provider, where he served as vice president of legal. In that role, he oversaw all legal aspects of project finance, including tax equity financings, tax credit sale agreements, securitizations, warehouse facilities, and other complex debt facilities supporting large-scale distributed energy deployment.

During his tenure, Sanocki held roles of increasing seniority while leading more than 50 tax equity financings totaling over $10 billion across distributed energy portfolios, with responsibilities spanning the drafting and negotiation of key transaction documents, preparation of critical deliverables, and overall legal oversight of each financing.

"Ken has operated at the center of the energy transition, helping shape the financing structures that enabled distributed energy to scale into mainstream infrastructure," said Doug Clark, managing partner of Wilson Sonsini. "He brings deep technical experience in tax equity and structured financings, along with the judgment and commercial instincts clients need in a rapidly evolving market. We are delighted to welcome him to the firm."

At Wilson Sonsini, Sanocki will advise companies, sponsors, and investors on structured and project finance across renewable energy, storage, and climate infrastructure. He will also support clients in capital formation and strategic transactions, including the development of portfolios of revenue-generating assets, as well as companies preparing for IPOs or pursuing M&A activity.

"I'm excited to join Wilson Sonsini's ECS practice, which has built a market-leading platform at the convergence of energy innovation, market-opening regulatory advocacy, and capital formation," said Sanocki. "The team has created something truly distinctive, and I look forward to working alongside them to help clients navigate complex financings and scale the next generation of energy infrastructure."

Prior to Sunrun, Sanocki served as senior counsel for global structured and project finance at SunEdison and began his career as a project finance associate at Milbank.

He earned his J.D. from New York University School of Law and his B.A. from Boston College.

Sanocki joins following last year's additions of partners Stanford (Stan) Renas, who focuses on structured finance and securitization transactions, and Matt Christiansen, the most recent former general counsel of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission. Together, these hires strengthen Wilson Sonsini's ECS platform and expand its capabilities in sophisticated, asset-based energy transition finance, reinforcing the firm's position as a market leader in clean energy and infrastructure advisory.

About Wilson Sonsini Goodrich &Rosati

For more than 60 years, Wilson Sonsini's services and legal disciplines have focused on serving the principal challenges faced by the management and boards of directors of business enterprises. The firm is nationally recognized as a leading provider to growing and established clients seeking legal counsel to complete sophisticated corporate and technology transactions; manage governance and enterprise-scale matters; assist with intellectual property development, protection, and IP-driven transactions; represent them in contested disputes; and/or advise them on antitrust or other regulatory matters. With deep roots in Silicon Valley, Wilson Sonsini has more than 1,000 attorneys and 17 offices in 16 technology, business, and regulatory markets across the United States, Asia, and Europe. For more information, please visit www.wsgr.com .

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260505836362/en/

value.Title: MAYOR WU CELEBRATES LOCAL ENVIRONMENTAL CHAMPIONS AT THE 2026 BOSTON CLIMATE LEADER AWARDS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGH-08M3-RSW5-42WB-00000-00MAYOR WU CELEBRATES LOCAL ENVIRONMENTAL CHAMPIONS AT THE 2026 BOSTON CLIMATE LEADER AWARDS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:31ZLexisNexisMAYOR WU CELEBRATES LOCAL ENVIRONMENTAL CHAMPIONS AT THE 2026 BOSTON CLIMATE LEADER AWARDS

BOSTON, May 2 -- The City of Boston issued the following press release:

BOSTON - April 29, 2026 - Mayor Michelle Wu and the City of Boston's Environment Department hosted the second annual Boston Climate Leader Awards at The Prudential Center's View Boston, honoring the local leaders making extraordinary contributions to the city's ambitious climate action goals. The evening united a diverse coalition of community advocates, business leaders, and civic partners to celebrate excellence in five key areas: buildings, energy, environmental protection, climate preparedness, and research and innovation.

"These Boston Climate Leader Award winners reflect the best of our City: the talent, innovation, and community-based work that is helping Boston thrive for generations to come," said Mayor Michelle Wu. "Their partnership is essential to advancing Boston's climate action plan and achieving our shared sustainability and resilience goals. I'm grateful for their dedication, which sets an example not only for our community, but for our nation and the world."

Awardees were selected for their sustainability impact, climate innovation, and benefits to Environmental Justice communities. This competitive, community-led process received 87 nominations for various individuals and organizations. Applicants went through a multi-stage evaluation process conducted by various City of Boston departments to ensure the highest standards of impact and innovation.

WinnCompanies has set a new standard for sustainable affordable housing by implementing deep energy retrofits and fossil-fuel-free construction across Environmental Justice neighborhoods, ensuring low-income residents benefit from increased comfort and reduced energy burdens.

Working towards portfolio-wide decarbonization for their 1,200 housing units, DBEDC's recent investment in rooftop solar installations is projected to offset 3.5 million kilograms of carbon dioxide over 25 years.

Yve established what is now known as the PowerCorps Building Operations track, creating essential career pathways for young adults while providing the building industry with the skilled workforce needed to meet ambitious energy reduction goals.

IT'S ELECTRIC is accelerating Boston's transition to zero-emission mobility by deploying compact, curbside EV chargers designed specifically for dense neighborhoods, removing the primary barrier to EV adoption for residents without private driveways.

The Green Energy Consumers Alliance addresses climate justice, supports local community aggregation programs, and informs residents across Greater Boston about energy alternatives, affordability, and positive change campaigns.

Bob Biggio has pioneered a national model for sustainable healthcare by integrating green infrastructure, such as solar arrays, rooftop farms, and a cogeneration power plant into Boston Medical Center's operations, reinvesting millions in saved utility costs back into patient care.

A global leader in ocean conservation, the New England Aquarium invests in cutting-edge scientific research, informs policy decisions and industry practices related to ocean conservation, and educates and inspires over 1 million visitors annually.

The BFFC is protecting land, increasing tree canopy, and increasing green space equity by transforming vacant lots in historically under-invested neighborhoods into public edible food forests.

For decades, Mela Bush-Miles has led the charge for clean air and transit equity in Environmental Justice communities, spearheading the Clean Buses for Boston campaign and serving as a pivotal force behind the electrification of the Fairmount-Indigo Line.

Resilient Boston Award - Recognizing leaders that are preparing Boston for the impacts of climate change, including sea level rise, severe weather events, and extreme heat.

As the largest employer in Massachusetts, Mass General Brigham advances community resilience through intentional healthcare and strategic investments in climate-ready infrastructure, including two new facilities built to withstand environmental hazards while doubling as public emergency shelters.

Serving as a unifier for waterfront residents and businesses, the Wharf District Council has spearheaded climate advocacy by developing a Conceptual Climate Resiliency Masterplan that features a 1.5-mile flood barrier designed to protect the downtown waterfront from sea-level rise while preserving its historic character.

Dr. Fabian's data-driven research and collaborative leadership in environmental health has directly advanced resilience, equity and public health policy within Boston's schools and frontline communities.

Through their Center for Energy Efficiency and the Trades, FC Tech is building a diverse pool of innovative thinkers to fill specialized roles that support the advancement of green technologies such as heat pumps, sustainable buildings, and electric power generation.

Through collaborative projects like Wicked Hot Boston and Wicked Hot Mystic, the Mystic River Watershed Association and the Museum of Science are providing foundational data needed to address extreme heat as a critical environmental justice issue.

Dr. Dafforn is reimagining Boston's coastline by installing habitat panels on existing seawall infrastructure to enhance marine biodiversity, improve water quality, and reduce coastal erosion.

Mayor Wu also honored Amos Hostetter Jr. with the Climate Catalyst Legacy Award, honoring him for his years of cultivating lasting public-private partnerships to drive climate action. Hostetter co-founded the Barr Foundation, which has funded a wide range of climate action projects across Boston. Hostetter also co-founded the Green Ribbon Commission with Mayor Thomas M. Menino, bringing together local institutions and businesses to accelerate the City's response to climate change.

"Achieving Boston's climate goals depends on local leadership in every neighborhood," said Brian Swett, Chief Climate Officer. "We know that bold climate action not only tackles an existential threat, but also provides a once-in-a-generation opportunity to deliver lower energy bills, good-paying jobs, and cleaner air for our communities. We are proud to celebrate these partners as we work together to accelerate our progress toward our shared sustainability and resilience goals."

"Climate Leader Awards is our opportunity to celebrate our local movers and shakers who are turning bold ideas into reality," said Oliver Sellers-Garcia, Environment Commissioner and Green New Deal Director. "These awardees are setting an example not just for our community, but the nation at-large, proving that community-centered climate work is in full swing here in Boston."

On Monday, April 27, 2026, Mayor Michelle Wu announced the release of Boston's landmark 2030 Climate Action Plan alongside dozens of community and institutional partners at LoPresti Park in East Boston. This implementation plan provides a roadmap for how the City will achieve its carbon emissions reduction and climate resilience goals, including reducing community-wide greenhouse gas emissions 50 percent by 2030 and reaching carbon neutrality by 2050.

urn:contentItem:6JHT-3V73-RX9Y-N05N-00000-00National Grid plc (LSE: NG) Leverages ASI AI To Optimize Grid Planning and Incident Response2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:30ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSNational Grid plc (LSE: NG) Leverages ASI AI To Optimize Grid Planning and Incident Response

BRITISH DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED KINGDOM EDITION

08 May 2026 09:16 BST

BOSTON, May 6, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Air Space Intelligence (ASI) and National Grid plc (LSE: NG) join forces to bring AI-driven decision support to electric grid planning and operations, effective from 7th May 2026. ASI will apply predictive modeling and scenario optimization to resilience planning, outage detection, and energy storage deployment, with the goal of reducing costs and time in the field.

ASI's leadership highlights the collaboration as a continuation of its work in mission-critical domains, while National Grid emphasizes the potential of AI to enhance decision-making across its U.S. grid operations.

The arrangement involves integrating grid topology, asset and reliability data, outages, forecasting, geospatial, and environmental data into a consistently updated predictive model of the grid. ASI will autonomously generate and optimize numerous planning scenarios to deliver practical recommendations for efficiency and reliability. Initial work targets siting and interconnection of distributed energy resources, resilience planning for extreme weather and demand surges, and more precise outage mapping, with Innovate Mass grant support from the Massachusetts Clean Energy Center.

National Grid today announced an interim dividend of GBX16.35 per share. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, November 20 and it is payable on Tuesday, January 13, 2026.

SECTION 2 NATIONAL GRID ACTIVITIES

Activities

National Grid PLC is a leading multinational utility company headquartered in the United Kingdom, with operations spanning the UK and northeastern United States. Its core business activities focus on the transmission and distribution of electricity and gas, ensuring reliable energy supply to over 20 million customers while maintaining critical infrastructure for national energy security. The company is actively investing in infrastructure to support the transition to a low-carbon economy, including grid modernization and renewable energy integration. Among its fastest-growing segments are offshore wind projects, which are expanding rapidly to meet rising demand for sustainable energy, as well as smart grid technologies and electric vehicle charging networks. These initiatives position National Grid as a key player in advancing energy efficiency and decarbonization, driving long-term growth through strategic investments and innovation. It is the largest Gas, water & multiutilities company by market capitalisation in the United Kingdom.

urn:contentItem:6JHS-3MB3-S1W3-C241-00000-00National Grid plc (LSE: NG) Collaborates With Air Space Intelligence on AI For Resilience, Outage Mapping and DER Siting2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:31ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSNational Grid plc (LSE: NG) Collaborates With Air Space Intelligence on AI For Resilience, Outage Mapping and DER Siting

BRITISH DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED KINGDOM EDITION

08 May 2026 03:16 BST

BOSTON, May 6, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Air Space Intelligence (ASI) is partnering with National Grid plc (LSE: NG) to apply AI-powered decision support to grid planning and operations, with the initiative taking effect from 7th May 2026. The collaboration aims to reduce field costs and shorten decision cycles by using ASI's predictive modeling and scenario optimization for resilience planning, outage detection, and energy storage deployment.

ASI's Buckendorf notes that the project builds on ASI's experience in critical environments and extends these capabilities to National Grid's grid. National Grid's Franklin says the tools will help convert diverse grid data into actionable insights to address emerging challenges.

The partnership will integrate multiple data streams—grid topology, asset and reliability data, outages, forecasts, geospatial and environmental data—into an evolving predictive model of the grid. The AI will generate and assess millions of planning scenarios to support decisions, with initial emphasis on siting and interconnection of distributed energy resources, resilience planning for weather and demand spikes, and improvements to outage mapping. The Massachusetts Clean Energy Center's Innovate Mass program backs the project.

value.Title: SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION - UPDATES FROM BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK1-XSF3-SHYX-40NF-00000-00SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION - UPDATES FROM BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKSUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION - UPDATES FROM BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

PermID5000317077Websitehttp://www.suffolk.com/IndustryConstruction & Engineering

ACTIVITIES:

Suffolk Construction, founded in 1982 by John Fish in Boston, Massachusetts, is a leading U.S. general contractor. With $5.5 billion in revenue and 2,500 employees, Suffolk delivers complex projects in healthcare, education, and commercial sectors. Notable works include Bostons Millennium Tower and Miamis Brickell City Centre. Employing advanced technologies like BIM and virtual design, Suffolk enhances project efficiency and safety. The company operates 10 offices nationwide, with a strong Northeast presence. Suffolks commitment to sustainability includes LEED-certified projects and eco-friendly practices. Its employee-centric culture, with robust training and diversity initiatives, earned it a spot on Fortunes Best Workplaces list. Suffolk continues to innovate, focusing on smart buildings and urban development.

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ VDOT REOPENS ROUTE 58 WESTBOUND; OVERNIGHT CLOSURES CONTINUE FOR SPSA WORK

As of 5th May 2026, the right lane on Route 58 westbound east of downtown Suffolk has reopened, restoring three westbound lanes.The move comes as Suffolk Construction completes parts of the Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project, which began in August 2024, with a focus on building a ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic to access the regional landfill without crossing westbound lanes.Motorists should expect ongoing restrictions, including long-term shoulder closures and occasional overnight work from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., depending on weather and scheduling. The zone's speed limit is 50 mph and speed cameras are in operation. Updates are available on VDOT's project site. Suffolk Construction says the milestone reflects progress in the critical infrastructure effort and the company's commitment to the region.

+ VMO2 EXPANDS RENEWABLE FOOTPRINT WITH EGG POWER SOLAR PPA

VMO2 has signed a 10-year PPA with Egg Power to source energy from a 70MW Suffolk solar farm under construction, with generation expected in 2027. The deal, announced on 1st May 2026, will supply about 5% of VMO2's energy needs. Egg Power and VMO2 are both Liberty Global-owned, aligning with the company's Net Zero by 2040 target.Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, said, "This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2's journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We're committed to cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK." The arrangement builds on a previous 10-year wind power deal with The Renewables Infrastructure Group (TRIG) signed last year; together these PPAs will help VMO2 source around 20% of its energy from renewables and support its sustainability goals.

+ SUFFOLK SETS OUT $897 MILLION 2027 CAPITAL BUDGET FOCUSED ON INFRASTRUCTURE PUSH

Suffolk County Executive Romaine unveiled a $897 million capital budget proposal for 2027, part of a roughly $3 billion three-year program, with a 15% increase over the adopted 2026 budget. The plan was released on 24th April 2026 and targets long term public works while operating independently from the county’s operating budget due in the fall.Highlights include $208 million for sewer upgrades, $200 million for a transportation hub near the Ronkonkoma LIRR station, and $128 million for road, bridge, sidewalk and public transit improvements.Additionally, $185 million would fund a new medical examiner building with better ventilation, security and laboratory facilities, while the Smith Point Bridge would be funded mainly with federal aid.Construction on the medical examiner facility would begin in 2027 after a $20 million bond approved in February 2025 for design and engineering, with completion targeted for 2029. Romaine said the budget would support farmland preservation, recreation and growth, and a working group will review it with May meetings after analysis by the budget office.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD CAROLINA AVIATION OPERATIONS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026, tasking him with leading and expanding the firm's aviation practice across North and South Carolina. With 19 years in construction and more than 12 years at Balfour Beatty, Johnson has delivered 22 aviation projects, including work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport. In his new role, he will support existing aviation clients and broaden the regional footprint at airports throughout the Carolinas, while emphasising safety, continuity, and effective coordination with stakeholders. He earned a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and participates in community initiatives such as the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK NAMED TO BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK, EXPANDING RAIL GEOTECHNICAL CAPABILITIES

Mainmark UK has joined the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, enabling it to support Network Rail’s CP7 programme from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an estimated £43–45 billion allocated across Great Britain.CP7 focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway, with priorities including maintenance, renewals, digital signaling, safety improvements, and enhanced freight and passenger performance.Mainmark UK will contribute through geotechnical response work, bridge repairs, structural projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks, under low complexity calls, preventative maintenance, and small civil schemes.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager of Mainmark UK, said, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans." Mainmark’s Teretek resin injection technology stabilises weak ground and fills voids without heavy excavation, helping maintain rail integrity as freight loads and weather events grow. The CP7 appointment signals a continued role for Mainmark in enhancing resilience and reliability of the UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY’S DUAL-MARKET STRATEGY DELIVERS PROFITABILITY AMID RISING COSTS

Balfour Beatty PLC reported solid growth, driven by its construction division as power-transmission work and US building projects supported the top line. The group’s market value rose roughly two-thirds over the last year as it benefits from a record order book, while underlying earnings per share increased 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the company said performance was aided by UK power-transmission demand and a pickup in US activity. Its support services unit lifted operating profit by a third to £122m, with the underlying margin improving by 80 basis points to 8.5%, and the UK construction arm hitting margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, higher project volumes helped offset cost overruns on a Civils project, underscoring the benefits of Balfour Beatty’s dual-market approach. Analysts remain optimistic about its outlook as the order book grows and infrastructure investment continues.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D AND HENSEL PHELPS ACCELERATE AI-DRIVEN ANALYTICS FOR LARGE-SCALE CONSTRUCTION

Track3D and Hensel Phelps announced a multi-year agreement to extend Track3D's Reality Intelligence across more than 200 active projects, moving beyond a pilot phase as of Apr. 1, 2026.The partnership aims to standardize portfolio-wide progress monitoring, enabling earlier detection of on-site issues and reducing delays on complex builds while boosting schedule reliability and oversight.A San Francisco International Airport case study demonstrated the platform's proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and involvement of field personnel, reinforcing its value in infrastructure work; a March update introduced an admin dashboard, Groups capability and capture-free floorplan status capture to streamline management.

+ HENSEL PHELPS ELEVATES MICAH CHOY TO LEAD SUPERINTENDENT ON REGIONAL KITCHEN PROJECT

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a milestone in his career with the firm, which he joined in 2018.In his tenure with Hensel Phelps, Choy has worked on notable projects in Hawaii and Guam across aviation, wastewater, and federal markets.His promotion underscores Hensel Phelps' focus on cultivating talent and positioning Choy to lead the Regional Kitchen initiative, which aims to enhance educational facilities in Hawaii.

+ TRACK3D AND HENSEL PHELPS SIGNAL SCALABLE AI IN CONSTRUCTION WITH 200-PROJECT DEPLOYMENT

As of 25th March 2026, Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, announced a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps that expands from a pilot to deployment on more than 200 active projects.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across its portfolio for progress monitoring, signaling confidence in the technology’s scalability across diverse project types. The rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue, strengthen customer loyalty, and create a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In a notable case, Track3D highlighted a San Francisco International Airport deployment where Hensel Phelps used the platform to shift from reactive to proactive decision-making, improving progress tracking and coordination across trades without major workflow changes. The company also detailed a March product release that adds an admin dashboard and Groups for centralized portfolio visibility, plus capture-free progress tracking on floorplans for easier site documentation.Track3D is pursuing its go-to-market strategy with a focus on specialty contractors and plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026, signaling momentum in its growth as it shifts toward automated production tracking and enhanced margins.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER REACHES STRUCTURAL MILESTONE AS TURNER MOVES TOWARD FIT-OUT

Turner Construction Company celebrated a major milestone at the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, with the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility on the 28th of April 2026. The project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours and involves about 345 skilled workers. The center spans 450,000 square feet on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel frame, an insulated precast exterior, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling system that blends liquid and air-cooled technologies. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," commented Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." With the structure now in place, attention turns to interior fit-out and readiness as the project progresses toward activation and continued support for the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER LEADS 11-STORY GEISINGER TOWER BUILD IN $900M VALLEY UPGRADE

Turner Construction has kicked off vertical construction on the $900 million Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center expansion in Wilkes-Barre, a milestone in one of the region's largest health care investments. Steel is rising on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot tower that will increase beds from 356 to 500 and add operating rooms from 12 to 18, while enlarging the emergency department and ensuring all private rooms. The project targets a first-quarter 2028 opening. This announcement was issued on 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, said the project represents expanded access and a modern health care platform for families across northeastern Pennsylvania, noting the company’s pride in partnering with Geisinger. The expansion is expected to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Peak onsite staffing could reach 450 workers, with Geisinger planning to hire 400 more after completion.Originally opened in 1981, the Wyoming Valley campus has grown to meet demand, and the new tower will bring capabilities on par with major academic medical centers serving large cities.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION TO SPEARHEAD WESTCOURT ORLANDO CONSTRUCTION IN MAJOR DOWNTOWN MIXED-USE SCHEME

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ ONTARIO GENERAL CONTRACTORS ASSOCIATION AWARDS SPOTLIGHT COLLABORATIVE STRUCTURES AND PCL

Collaborative Structures Limited won four awards at the OGCA Builder Awards, presented during a ceremony on the 17th of April, 2026, for outstanding work on the AIM Recycling facility in Kitchener and Conestoga College's Indigenous Classrooms renovation. PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. captured three honors for the Scotiabank Arena Venue Reimagination Phase 2, the Rogers Centre Renovation, and Limberlost Place, highlighting its role in advancing Ontario infrastructure and renovation projects.The OGCA Builder Awards recognize projects that exemplify innovation, safety, and overall excellence, with prize values spanning from 5 million to over 300 million. The Scotiabank Arena Phase 2 project is valued at 150 to 300 million and aims to enhance fan experiences, with completion planned for April 2025. The Rogers Centre Renovation, exceeding 300 million, concentrates on seating and player facilities. Limberlost Place stands as Ontario's first institutional mass timber building, reflecting a commitment to sustainable construction, with PCL collaborating with George Brown Polytechnic to promote mass timber education. Geoff Howe of PCL emphasized delivering client visions while navigating the unique challenges of each project.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION HONORED ON FORTUNE'S 100 BEST LIST FOR 2026

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been named to Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For for 2026, the official announcement dated 2nd April 2026. The recognition highlights PCL's commitment to fostering a high-trust workplace and strong employee engagement.It marks the 17th time PCL has earned a spot on the list, with a No. 91 ranking for 2026. The designation is linked to the company's employee-owned structure, which helps cultivate a culture where staff feel valued and motivated.Fortune's list is widely viewed as a gauge of organizational health and employee experience, often correlating with stronger business performance.In addition, PCL’s year also featured an Engineering News-Record ranking, placing No. 11 on the Top 400 Contractors list for 2025, underscoring leadership in delivering complex projects across North America while prioritizing people.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION LEADS INFRASTRUCTURE SOLUTIONS FOR COLORADO RIVER WATER SECURITY

On the 26th of March 2026, the Colorado River's drought conditions are tightening supplies across the West.Lake Powell is 162 feet below full and holds about 26% of its capacity; the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a reassessment of basin-wide water management.PCL Construction is pushing for expanded water reuse infrastructure as a central resilience tool, aligning with a projected national average of $47.1 billion in water reuse investments over the next decade. Adoption varies among the seven basin states.With the fall 2026 expiration looming, PCL's Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix provides a model for maintaining reliable supplies amid growth, and participants at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference highlighted the need for a multifaceted approach.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE TO INVEST $64.6 MILLION IN BIRMINGHAM HQ UPGRADE BACKED BY COUNTY GRANT

On 25th March 2026, the Jefferson County Commission approved a jobs grant to Brasfield & Gorrie to back a Birmingham expansion that includes a new office tower, a parking deck expansion, a training and event center, and a wellness center.The expansion involves a total investment of $64,584,500 and would create 80 new jobs with an hourly wage of $39.51, supported by a county grant capped at $240,000.Brasfield & Gorrie’s plan includes land purchase of $2,884,500, construction at $56,100,000, and equipment at $5,600,000. The program also calls for a return of $8,000 to the general fund and $1,011,773 to the education fund.

+ BIRMINGHAM HQ EXPANSION PLAN: FOUR-STORY CAMPUS BUILDING, PARKING DECK UPGRADE, AND LOCAL JOBS

Brasfield &Gorrie unveiled its plan to expand its Birmingham national headquarters, presenting it to the city council committee on 10th March 2026, as part of a long-term growth strategy for the city.The project would add a four-story, roughly 80,000-square-foot building on the HQ campus along 7th Avenue South, with the first floor serving as an event and training center featuring a banquet hall, an auditorium, and training rooms.The upper floors would be devoted to office uses to support national operations, and a fourth level would be added to the existing parking deck to provide about 125 more spaces.The deal contemplates 80 new Birmingham-based full-time jobs with an average salary around $82,000; authorities would provide incentives up to $80,000; completion possible by late 2027 if approved.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE ELEVATES MIKE FOUSHEE TO HEAD CAROLINAS OPERATIONS

Brasfield & Gorrie has named Mike Foushee to lead the Carolinas operations, with the appointment taking effect from 10th February 2026. He will be based in the Charlotte office and oversee strategy and operations for the Raleigh and Greenville offices.A Greenville native, Foushee has spent more than two decades with Brasfield & Gorrie advancing through operational leadership roles, most recently as regional president in Atlanta where he directed teams on large projects such as Science Square at Georgia Tech and Omni Hotels developments.Foushee will prioritize expanding Brasfield & Gorrie’s presence across commercial, healthcare and industrial sectors in the Carolinas, backed by the firm’s turnkey delivery model that covers site selection, in-house design, procurement and MEP management to accelerate schedules and mitigate risk.The Carolinas portfolio includes landmark projects like Ally Charlotte Center and the Carroll A. Campbell Jr. U.S. Courthouse in Greenville, and Foushee will also continue the company’s community investment and workforce development initiatives in the region.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN DEBUTS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ELEVATE PROJECT EFFICIENCY

JE Dunn Construction has unveiled Form Off-Site Solutions, a new off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex components. The launch occurred 6th April 2026, signaling the company’s intent to advance its Kansas City legacy into a wider market.Led by Nick Effenheim, the team merges skilled craftspeople with automation to produce metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies.The service model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, safety, and overall quality while helping reduce costs and shorten timelines.Effenheim notes, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and turn ideas into a manufactured reality is what sets Form apart." The Kansas City and Nashville facilities position Form for national work.

+ NORTHLAND WORKFORCE CENTER ADVANCES WITH TOPPING-OUT CEREMONY

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — JE Dunn Construction marked a topping-out milestone for the Northland Workforce Development Center on 19th March 2026, with the final steel beam installed on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.A beam-signing ceremony drew attendees who left signatures and messages on a beam bearing project and partner logos, underscoring collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, stakeholders, and the community. Project Executive Brad Hokanson commented, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partners to serve high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training areas, and technology-rich environments that mirror real-world workplaces.The project, located in the Northland region of Kansas City, is on schedule with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated after the milestone. JE Dunn is serving as construction manager in partnership with the district and Clark & Enersen, prioritizing safety, coordination, and ongoing communication with stakeholders to minimize disruption to nearby facilities.

+ CHELETTE RETURNS TO JE DUNN TO LEAD HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION GROWTH IN THE SOUTHEAST

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE [3 News That Matters]

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ELEVATES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS TO HEAD OF CAPITAL STRATEGY

As of 28th April 2026, Gilbane Development named Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets, based in the firm's New York City office.He will refine and execute the funding strategy, reporting to president and CEO James Patchett, and will help guide capital strategy and investment decisions while building relationships with funding and equity partners.Vlahakis previously served as managing director for capital markets at Thor Equities, where he led equity-raising efforts for a range of projects and partnerships. His prior role as senior vice president and chief of staff at Thor Equities highlights his breadth of financial leadership.Gilbane says the appointment strengthens its capital program and supports its growth plans in core sectors.

+ GILBANE-WKU PPP AIMS TO MODERNIZE HOUSING, EXPAND STUDENT RESOURCES

WKU announced the approval of a public-private partnership with Gilbane to develop a new residence hall replacing Keen and Poland Halls, with the Board of Regents' approval on 11th December 2025. The Kentucky Legislature's approval on 6th April 2026 clears the way for construction. SGA Senator Barker stated the partnership is fully authorized and that WKU is now ready to begin building. The SGA also advanced measures to benefit students, including Resolution 7-26-S for a crosswalk near housing and collaboration with the Kentucky DOT, and Bill 17-26-S providing $50 reimbursements for textbooks, along with provisions for Scantrons and lab goggles.

+ DC AFFORDABLE HOUSING EXPANSION: GILBANE STARTS LEASING FOR 229-UNIT PHASE II

Gilbane Development has initiated leasing for Phase II of its Barnaby & 7th redevelopment in Southeast Washington, D.C., delivering 229 units across four new buildings, The Stoneview, The Solomon, The Marte, and The Lewis. As of 3rd April 2026, the project is on track for completion by summer 2026 and features a mix of studios through three-bedroom units serving households at 50% and 80% of AMI, with 20% of units set aside as Permanent Supportive Housing under DC DHS.Phase I, The Upland, was completed in Spring 2025 and includes 169 apartments, including 34 PSH units. Income restrictions for Phase I range from 30% to 50% of AMI.Phase III is expected to add about 100 additional rental units in one multifamily building, bringing the grand total to 498 units upon completion. A pedestrian walkway now connects all six buildings in the larger development, and DC Housing Finance Agency places Phase II's cost at roughly $135 million.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES TAKES HELM OF HR FOR NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING AT CLARK CONSTRUCTION

Carly Reiner Caceres has assumed the role of Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, effective Apr. 29, 2026, tasked with coordinating nationwide campus initiatives to expand early-career talent pipelines for the company.Her career blends leadership in People Operations at Proper, where she guided HR strategy in a hybrid environment, with roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and nearly a decade at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ experience spans full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder engagement, and employer branding, capabilities Clark Construction aims to leverage to attract top talent from universities across the United States. She has also led campus recruiting efforts at Grant Thornton LLP, strengthening internship programs and student engagement.Clark Construction, founded in 1906, emphasizes community impact and project excellence. Caceres’ background in communications and external relations from early roles at Charleston Magazine and the Hirshhorn Museum is expected to support her work in nurturing the next generation of construction professionals.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS TO DELIVER REPAIRS, MAINTENANCE AND REFURBISHMENT UNDER RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL FRAMEWORK

Clark Contracts has been named to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades framework to improve repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties in the area. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to better manage both responsive and planned maintenance across its housing stock and to deliver more timely service to residents.Clark Contracts is already active in the public sector, with commitments including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework, and involvement with Crown Commercial Service and Scottish Procurement Alliance projects.Gordon Cunningham, managing director, noted the company’s track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over three years and said the appointment strengthens the services available to Renfrewshire residents.

+ THE VILLAGES SET FOR ₱4-B MIXED-USE PROJECT AS CDC ENDORSES 30-MONTH BUILD

Clark Development Corp. has approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease for a ₱4-billion mixed-use hub at The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination in Clark Freeport. The amendment grants PKR a 30-month timeframe to construct the project and introduces performance safeguards to help ensure on-schedule delivery. CDC president and CEO Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera described the move as reinforcing investor confidence and the community’s integration within The Villages, while PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership and pledged timely completion. PKR operates under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean builder with recognized strengths in safety, technology, and workplace practices. The development aligns with CDC’s strategy to accelerate investments, foster well-planned communities, and strengthen Clark as a business-friendly destination that attracts both local and international capital.
